ActionItems: Lock in a two-year lease before the rent step-up and move one wholesale account to prepaid weekly billing to protect cash flow.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Break-even review for Koa Morning Star Bakery ahead of the Kaimuki storefront lease renewal, comparing current margins against two proposed rent increases.

CurrentState.Note: At about $14k monthly gross the bakery clears roughly $4.4k after costs; Roger draws $4,200 and keeps $200 in reserves. Payroll for Juno, Chloe and Tyler plus ingredients from Y. Hata, Meadow Gold, and Hawaiian Crown are the largest costs.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Even at the higher $1,850 rent the bakery stays above break-even, but monthly net drops to about $4,200 — leaving almost no cushion for reserves unless counter volume grows past roughly 31 covers a day.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed and variable costs of $9,700 a month over an average $12.50 ticket and 26 trading days works out to about 30 paying customers a day to break even.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Adds $100/month in rent; net income slips from $4,400 to $4,300, still comfortably covering Roger's draw.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent rises to $1,750 at lease renewal

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Adds $200/month in rent; net income falls to $4,200, matching Roger's draw and leaving nothing for reserves that month.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Rent rises to $1,850 at lease renewal
